title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The tool itself does not pose a direct security threat but could be misused by attackers to identify vulnerabilities in public or private repositories. Additionally, if the tool has its own vulnerabilities, it could become a target for exploitation.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before adoption to ensure it meets organizational standards.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback for any reported vulnerabilities or misuse cases.
Educate developers and security teams on the proper use of the tool to avoid accidental exposure of sensitive data.

title: The Auto Image Attributes From Filename With Bulk Updater (Add Alt Text, Image Title For Image...
description: The Auto Image Attributes From Filename With Bulk Updater (Add Alt Text, Image Title For Image SEO) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the attachment metadata in all versions up to, and including, 4.9 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3722.

title: A buffer overflow vulnerability in the UPnP AddPortMapping() command in Zyxel VMG4005-B50B...
description: A buffer overflow vulnerability in the UPnP AddPortMapping() command in Zyxel VMG4005-B50B firmware versions through 5.13(ABRL.5.4)C0 could allow an adjacent attacker to trigger a temporary denial-of-service (DoS) condition affecting the UPnP function of the affected device.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in the UPnP AddPortMapping() command could allow an attacker on the same network to cause a temporary denial-of-service (DoS) condition, disrupting network services and device functionality.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware update from Zyxel to patch the vulnerability (check for updates beyond version 5.13(ABRL.5.4)C0).
Disable UPnP functionality on affected devices if it is not required for operations.
Implement network segmentation to minimize exposure of vulnerable devices to untrusted or adjacent attackers.

title: A vulnerability was detected in itsourcecode Fees Management System 1.0. Affected is an unknown...
description: A vulnerability was detected in itsourcecode Fees Management System 1.0. Affected is an unknown function of the file /manage_payment.php. The manipulation of the argument ID results in sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the itsourcecode Fees Management System 1.0 allows for SQL injection attacks via the 'ID' parameter in the /manage_payment.php file. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database manipulation, or even full system compromise if exploited.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to sanitize user inputs in the affected application.
Update the Fees Management System to the latest version if a patch is available or contact the vendor for a security fix.
Monitor application logs for any suspicious activity, particularly unauthorized access attempts or SQL errors, and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block malicious requests.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function gmm_state_security_mode of the file src/amf/gmm-sm.c of the component NGAP Handover. Performing a manipulation results in race condition. The attack can be initiated remotely. The complexity of an attack is rather high. The exploitability is regarded as difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The pull request to fix this issue awaits acceptance.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.6 could allow attackers to exploit a race condition in the NGAP Handover component, potentially leading to service disruption or unauthorized access. However, the attack complexity is high, and exploitability is difficult, reducing the likelihood of widespread exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch once the pull request is accepted and the fix is officially released.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity indicating potential exploitation attempts.
Review and implement additional security measures for NGAP Handover processes to mitigate risks.

title: The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the color settings fields (Page Background, Form Background, Text Color, Link Color) in versions up to and including 1.0.3. This is due to insufficient input sanitization of the color option values (they were registered with register_setting() and stored via the Settings API/update_option() with no sanitize_callback) combined with the values being output into a <style> block on wp-login.php using e

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts via the color settings fields, which can lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or compromise of user accounts on WordPress sites using the affected plugin.
recommended_actions: Update the Simple Custom Login Page plugin to the latest version if a patch is available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts.
Audit and sanitize user input fields to prevent injection vulnerabilities.

title: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in...
description: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in versions 7.0.0 - 7.0.14, via the 'slider.get.full' AJAX Action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to extract sensitive data including raw social media API credentials: the Instagram OAuth token, Flickr API key, YouTube Data API key, and Facebook App ID, stored in any configured slider's settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Contributor-level access or higher to extract sensitive API credentials, potentially leading to unauthorized access to social media accounts and data leakage.
recommended_actions: Update the Slider Revolution plugin to the latest version (7.0.15 or higher) to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict Contributor-level access and review user permissions to minimize exposure to potential attackers.
Audit API keys and OAuth tokens stored in the plugin settings, and regenerate compromised credentials if necessary.

title: CVE-2026-9538 - Archive::Tar versions before 3.10 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via attacker controlled entry siz
description:
Archive::Tar versions before 3.10 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via attacker controlled entry size field in tar header.

_read_tar() reads each entry's payload with $handle->read($$data, $block), where $block is derived from the entry's 12-byte size field in the tar header with no upper bound on that value.

A crafted header declaring a multi-gigabyte size causes Perl to allocate a scalar of that size.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9538.

Upgrading to version 5.2.5 is able to address this issue. The name of the patch is 66d16516e24893bebc1c8af52bf2fe9ad0735061. Upgrading the affected component is advised.

title: CVE-2026-42497 - Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract hardlinks to attacker controlled paths outside th
description:
Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract hardlinks to attacker controlled paths outside the extraction directory.

_make_special_file() passes the tar header's linkname to link() without validating it against absolute paths or .. segments, creating a hardlink that shares the victim file's inode.

A subsequent write through the extracted name modifies the victim file, and the post-extraction chmod, chown, and utime block in _extract_file() (guarded only against symlinks via -l) applies the tar header's mode, owner, and timestamps to the shared inode during extraction alone.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42497.

title: CVE-2026-42496 - Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract symlinks with attacker controlled targets outside
description:
Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract symlinks with attacker controlled targets outside the extraction directory.

_make_special_file() passes the tar header's linkname to symlink() without validating it against absolute paths or .. segments. The secure-extract mode check that guards regular file extraction does not cover the symlink target.

A subsequent open through the extracted name reads or writes the attacker chosen path.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42496.

title:
CVE-2025-71310 - The GDPR cookies module for Backdrop CMS (before 

1.x-1.3.5) doesn't sufficiently protect visitors
description:
The GDPR cookies module for Backdrop CMS (before 

1.x-1.3.5) doesn't sufficiently protect visitors from Cross Site Scripting (XSS) if a malicious value has been provided for the optional 'Info content' field for the YouTube service. This is mitigated by the fact that an attacker must have a role with the permission "Create a GDPR Cookies Service" or "Edit any GDPR Cookies Service" and a site must have added a YouTube service as configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71310.

title: CVE-2026-8376 - Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a
description:
Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a repeated fixed string on 32-bit builds.

Perl_study_chunk in regcomp_study.c checked the size of the joined substring buffer in characters rather than bytes. For a quantified fixed substring with a large minimum count, the byte length mincount * l could overflow SSize_t, producing an undersized SvGROW allocation; the subsequent copy writes past the end of the buffer.

A caller that compiles an attacker-controlled regular expression on a 32-bit perl build triggers a heap buffer overflow at compile time.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8376.
